meta.title: 11.4 Change Data Capture
meta.description: Capturing database changes as events. Debezium, log-based CDC, and keeping systems in sync.

blocks.prompt: Your PostgreSQL database is the source of truth. Elasticsearch needs to stay in sync for search. Redis needs to stay in sync for caching. How do you propagate changes reliably?

blocks.content:
## Change Data Capture (CDC)

Capture changes from a databases transaction log and stream them as events.

```
PostgreSQL → WAL → Debezium → Kafka → Elasticsearch
                                     → Redis
                                     → Analytics DB
```

### How it works

**Log-based CDC (preferred):** Read the databases write-ahead log (WAL/binlog). Every INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE is captured without modifying the application.

**Trigger-based CDC:** Database triggers write changes to an outbox table. More portable but adds overhead.

**Timestamp-based CDC:** Query for rows modified since last check. Misses deletes. Not reliable.

### Debezium

The standard open-source CDC tool. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQL Server. Runs as a Kafka Connect connector.

**Events include:** Before/after state, operation type (insert/update/delete), timestamp, transaction ID.

### Use cases

- **Search indexing:** Database → Elasticsearch
- **Cache invalidation:** Database → Redis (invalidate on change)
- **Analytics:** OLTP database → analytics warehouse
- **Microservice sync:** One services database → another services read model
- **Event-driven architecture:** Turn database changes into domain events

### CDC vs dual writes

| | CDC | Dual writes |
|---|---|---|
| Consistency | Guaranteed (single source) | Risk of partial failure |
| Application changes | None (reads DB log) | Must modify write code |
| Latency | Seconds | Immediate |
| Complexity | Kafka + Debezium infra | Code complexity |

CDC captures database changes from the transaction log and streams them as events — no application changes needed. Debezium + Kafka is the standard stack. Use CDC instead of dual writes to keep multiple systems in sync reliably.
